# Engagement Brief - <TITLE>

> The PM's intake summary. Produced at the start of every engagement, before work begins,
> and updated as decisions are made. Authored in `.md`, rendered to `.html`. Created
> **together with** `artifacts/START-HERE.md` (the living index, status ⏳) - the brief is
> the opening bookend, the index is the state record a reader checks first.

> **Document control** · ID `ENG-001` · Version `0.1` · Status `Draft | In review | Approved`
> · Classification `Internal | Confidential` · Owner `<name / role>` · As-of `<YYYY-MM-DD>`
>
> | Version | Date | Author | Change |
> |---|---|---|---|
> | 0.1 | <YYYY-MM-DD> | <author> | Initial draft |

| | |
|---|---|
| **Engagement** | <short name> |
| **Requested by** | <user> |
| **Date** | <YYYY-MM-DD> |
| **Type** | discovery (idea→solution) / review / build-from-requirements |
| **Status** | intake / agreed / in delivery / delivered |

## 1. The ask (PM's restatement)
What the user wants, in the PM's own words, to confirm shared understanding.

## 2. Regulatory obligation
The specific obligation(s) this engagement addresses. Cite the article/rule (e.g. MAR Art.12,
6AMLD Art.18, FCA SYSC 10A, SEC 17a-4). If the engagement is not obligation-driven, state
"internal / non-regulatory" and record the business driver.

| Obligation | Jurisdiction | Article / rule | What it requires |
|------------|-------------|----------------|-----------------|
| <e.g. MAR Art.12> | EU | <specific article> | <plain-English summary> |

## 3. Agreed success criteria
What "done" looks like for this engagement. Agree these with the user before work begins -
never proceed without at least a draft. Criteria must be measurable or observable.

| # | Success criterion | How measured / verified | Agreed by |
|---|-------------------|-------------------------|-----------|
| 1 | <e.g. BRD and FSD produced, reviewed and signed off> | <documents exist, sign-offs captured> | <user / sponsor> |
| 2 | <e.g. detection scenario passes UAT with zero Critical defects> | <UAT sign-off record> | |
| 3 | <e.g. RTM updated end-to-end: obligation → requirement → code → test> | <RTM review by compliance-reviewer> | |

## 4. Decisions taken
Material decisions agreed with the user so far.

## 5. Open questions / clarifications needed
Questions the PM has put to the user and is awaiting answers on. **Do not guess these.**

## 6. Assumptions & constraints
What we're assuming in the absence of an answer; known constraints (time, data, scope).

## 7. Documentary artifacts requested
The deliverables the user selected from the menu (see WAYS-OF-WORKING.md), e.g. BRD, FSD,
review report, RTM, audit pack. Each produced in `.md` + `.html`.

## 8. Proposed approach & routing plan
The entry point and the chain of specialists, e.g. *business-analyst → SME →
rules-developer → code-reviewer → compliance-reviewer*. Be flexible - only the stages this
engagement needs.

## 9. Risks & dependencies
What could derail delivery; what we depend on.

## 10. Next action
The immediate next step and who owns it.

## Sign-off
| Role | Name | Decision | Date |
|------|------|----------|------|
| Author / owner | | | |
| `compliance-reviewer` (DoD gate) | | | |
| Human approver (or `[IT team]`) | | | |
